title: cassiope mertensiana subsp. gracilis
intro: Cassiope mertensiana subsp. gracilis, also known as the mountain or rock azalea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family. It is native to high-altitude areas in the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of Cassiope mertensiana subsp. gracilis can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
